channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sierra Leone accepts West African migrants deported from the US
item.description: On Wednesday, Sierra Leone received its first group of migrants deported from the United States under the former US administration’s immigration policies. A plane carrying nine individuals from Nigeri...

item.title: Troost-Ekong Says Winning Away Matches Crucial For AFCON 2027 Qualification
item.description: Former Super Eagles captain William Troost-Ekong don yan say the way teams take perform for away matches go fit really affect how dem go qualify for Africa Cup of Nations 2027. Troost-Ekong talk this ...

item.title: Ongoing Tourist Damage to Europe's Historic Landmarks Raises Local Concerns
item.description: Across Europe, a growing number of incidents involving tourists damaging historic landmarks is alarming both residents and heritage experts. Visitors have been observed carving initials into iconic st...

item.title: Mulamwah Misses Call from Daughter Keilah While Travelling to China, Shows Family Peace
item.description: Kenyan comedian Mulamwah recently shared a screenshot of a missed call from his daughter Keilah Oyando while preparing for a two-week seminar in China. Mulamwah, who is a father of three, had experien...

item.title: Zanzibar Lawmakers Criticise Outsourced Hospitals Over Poor Service and Staff Treatment
item.description: Zanzibar lawmakers have raised serious concerns about the performance of government hospitals that are run under private management contracts. Despite the government's investment in modern hospital in...

item.title: Annie Kilner hosts baby shower as she prepares to welcome fifth child, a daughter, with Kyle Walker
item.description: Annie Kilner, wife of footballer Kyle Walker, has shared images from her baby shower on Instagram as the couple await the arrival of their fifth child together, their first daughter. The 33-year-old p...

item.title: City of Cape Town plans affordable housing and mixed-use development on Civic Centre parking site
item.description: The City of Cape Town has approved preliminary plans to transform a municipal parking lot in the CBD's Foreshore precinct into an affordable housing and mixed-use development. Located next to the Civi...

item.title: Court dismisses union cases against St Mary’s Mumias Mission Hospital over staff disputes
item.description: The Employment and Labour Relations Court has dismissed two cases brought by the Kenya Union of Domestic, Hotels, Educational Institutions and Hospital Workers (Kudheiha) against St Mary’s Mumias Miss...

item.title: FAO warns of potential global food price increases due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions
item.description: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has issued a warning that disruptions caused by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz could lead to rising global food prices within the next s...

item.title: Zamboanga City restricts poultry and livestock imports amid avian flu cases in nearby towns
item.description: Zamboanga City has temporarily banned the entry of poultry and livestock products from specific towns in Zamboanga Sibugay after confirmed cases of avian influenza were reported. Mayor Khymer Olaso si...

item.title: Iran Warns of Regional Escalation if Attacks Resume
item.description: On May 20, 2026, Iran’s Revolutionary Guards issued a stern warning that any renewed military aggression by the United States or Israel could trigger a conflict extending far beyond West Asia. This st...

item.title: Madison Square Garden Bans Lawyer Representing Injured NYPD Officer Amid Legal Dispute
item.description: John Scola, an attorney known for representing NYPD officers, has been banned from Madison Square Garden (MSG) and other James Dolan-owned venues after filing a lawsuit on behalf of Officer John Przyb...

item.title: Keir Starmer mistakenly mentions North Korea instead of South Korea during PMQs
item.description: During Prime Minister’s Questions on 20 May, Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer made an unintended slip while discussing trade deals. Responding to Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey, Starmer listed tra...

item.title: Severe thunderstorms expected to end record heat wave in Washington DC area
item.description: A severe thunderstorm watch has been issued for the Washington DC area as an intense cold front approaches, promising to bring an end to the current record-setting heat wave. Meteorologists from the C...

item.title: Penny Penny joins Mayibuye to lead campaigns in Limpopo ahead of local elections
item.description: Xitsonga musician and former MP Gezani Erik Nkovani, popularly known as Papa Penny, has officially joined the Afrika Mayibuye Movement (Mayibuye), expressing that the party offers him a better platfor...

item.title: Eva Gabor's Hollywood Career and Style in the 1950s
item.description: Eva Gabor, born on February 11, 1919, in Hungary, became a celebrated Hungarian-American actress and socialite. During the 1950s, she established herself as a versatile performer in Hollywood and on B...

item.title: Worker Dies After Crane Collapse at Mindoro Bridge Construction Site
item.description: In Abra de Ilog, Occidental Mindoro, a tragic accident occurred on Tuesday afternoon when a crane collapsed at an ongoing bridge construction site, resulting in the death of a 61-year-old worker. Acco...

item.title: Khuzdar By-Election Postponed Over Security Concerns, Balochistan Govt Advises ECP
item.description: The Balochistan government has informed the Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) that holding the by-election for NA-256 Khuzdar is currently not feasible because of security concerns. The chief secr...

item.title: Perth mother runs for retinoblastoma awareness after son’s rare eye cancer diagnosis
item.description: A Perth mother, Emily Cross, has been thrust into a challenging journey after noticing a white reflection in her one-year-old son Peter’s eye. What initially seemed like a minor concern turned out to ...

item.title: Wickham Hosts Annual 750-Year-Old Horse Fair as Travellers Descend on Village
item.description: The Hampshire village of Wickham has once again become the centre of attention as hundreds of Gypsy and Roma travellers arrived for the annual Wickham Horse Fair, a tradition dating back 750 years. Th...
